messages.content:
HOW YOUR CLAIM BECOMES A NUMBER (the perturbation contract):
- You never state € impacts. You emit machine-readable ops; the engine computes
  ΔTAM/ΔSAM/ΔYAM by re-running the real funnel. Any € figure you write in prose
  will be stripped.
- Ops: {nodeId, op: "set"|"scale"|"add", value} rewrite one leaf's value;
  {nodeId, op: "exclude"} removes a dimension cell from the venture's reachable
  scope ("unavailable to us" — foreclosure), which is different from shrinking it.
- Ops apply in array order. Ratio nodes must stay inside [0,1]; tamBase must stay > 0.
- Only FUNNEL-ACTIVE nodes may be perturbed (marked in the ledger you receive).
  shape.cagr and shape.cr3 are context — a demand-pace or concentration risk must
  be translated into the funnel nodes it would actually move (tamBase level,
  serviceableFactor reach, obtainableFactor pace, or a share).
- Sibling shares are NOT re-normalized when you move one share (documented v1
  simplification). If you mean a mix-SHIFT, perturb multiple siblings; if you
  mean "this slice is smaller in absolute terms", one op is correct.
- Perturbing an assumption leaf overrides whatever the user set: your claim is
  about the world, not about their slider.

Fix the hypothesis's targetNodes/perturbation so it passes validation WITHOUT changing the claim's meaning. If the claim cannot be expressed against funnel-active nodes, translate it into the funnel nodes it would actually move.

FUNNEL-ACTIVE NODES:
tamBase (EUR_M, value 300)
geo.DE (ratio, value 0.5)
geo.NL (ratio, value 0.28)
geo.PL (ratio, value 0.07)
geo.CH (ratio, value 0.08)
geo.CZ (ratio, value 0.02)
geo.AT (ratio, value 0.02)
geo.other (ratio, value 0.03)
seg.hyperscale (ratio, value 0.44)
seg.colocation (ratio, value 0.18)
seg.enterprise (ratio, value 0.29)
seg.edge (ratio, value 0.05)
seg.telecom (ratio, value 0.04)
cust.operator-large (ratio, value 0.4)
cust.operator-mid (ratio, value 0.25)
cust.oem (ratio, value 0.22)
cust.distributor (ratio, value 0.13)
serviceableFactor (ratio, value 0.55)
obtainableFactor (ratio, value 0.01)

HYPOTHESIS:
{
 "id": "colo-denominator-misallocates-geography",
 "title": "Normalizing colo-only shares to 1.00 launders a scope mismatch: the €300M is geographically misallocated away from where PDU demand actually sits",
 "narrative": "Every geo.* share divides colo MW by colo MW, but rack-PDU demand tracks total installed IT load — and the gap between the two scopes is large and uneven by country. Germany's installed load (>2,700MW BMWK, 2,980MW Bitkom) is ~1,000MW above its colo figure; Switzerland's installed base (850.6MW) is 3x its colo number; Poland's installed-load figure (660MW) is corroborated by three independent sources versus one Statista colo point. Re-derived on installed load, DE falls toward 0.42 while CH and PL roughly double. TAM barely moves — which is exactly the trap: the headline is stable while the country-level plan (sales hires, certifications, distributor selection) is calibrated to the wrong map. Because the shares are normalized to sum 1.00, the axis looks internally consistent and no single node flags the systematic bias.",
 "category": "model-structure",
 "targetNodes": [
  "geo.DE",
  "geo.CH",
  "geo.PL"
 ],
 "mechanism": [
  {
   "step": "trigger",
   "text": "EUDCA-scale evidence confirms colo+hyperscale is only ~two-thirds of Europe's IT power, so a colo-only apportionment omits a third of PDU-consuming load — and the omitted share varies by country (self-operation-heavy CH and AT vs colo-heavy NL)",
   "refs": [
    "digest:geo-region-denominator#c11",
    "ledger:geo.CH"
   ]
  },
  {
   "step": "propagation",
   "text": "Country ratios re-derived on installed load diverge from the curated values: Poland's 660MW installed figure (Mordor, ResearchAndMarkets, Prime East all agree) yields ~0.10 vs curated 0.07; Switzerland's 850.6MW yields ~0.13 vs 0.08; Germany's own crossCheck already lands at 0.42 vs curated 0.50",
   "refs": [
    "digest:geo-PL#c0",
    "digest:geo-PL#c15",
    "digest:geo-alpine#c0",
    "ledger:geo.DE"
   ]
  },
  {
   "step": "model-impact",
   "text": "The geography axis reallocates: the Germany-first assumption embedded in geo.DE=0.50 overweights DE by up to 8 points of TAM while underweighting the two countries where the entrant faces the least incumbent framework density — a wrong-map error the normalized, clean-summing shares conceal",
   "refs": [
    "ledger:geo.DE",
    "ledger:geo.PL",
    "ledger:geo.CH"
   ]
  }
 ],
 "whyMissable": "The shares sum neatly to 1.00, DE and NL carry 'triangulated' maturity, and the axis passes every internal consistency check. Normalization is doing the hiding: it makes a colo-scoped numerator over a colo-scoped denominator look like a validated market map, when both sides of the fraction omit 35-40% of the demand-bearing load — unevenly.",
 "expectedObviousness": "low",
 "falsifier": "A single consistent-scope installed-IT-load country table across CE-7 (e.g., PMR series or EUDCA 2026 country profiles) confirming DE at ~50% and PL/CH at ≤0.08/0.09 of the regional total.",
 "likelihood": {
  "value": 0.5,
  "rationale": "The DE, CH, and PL crossChecks in the ledger itself already point the same direction (0.42, 0.13, 0.10), and the PL installed-load figure is triple-corroborated; the open question is only the magnitude of the reallocation, not its sign.",
  "basis": "evidence"
 },
 "perturbation": [
  {
   "nodeId": "geo.DE",
   "op": "set",
   "value": 0.42,
   "note": "Installed-load method (BMWK crossCheck) — mix-shift leg 1"
  },
  {
   "nodeId": "geo.CH",
   "op": "set",
   "value": 0.13,
   "note": "Installed-base method (Mordor 850.6MW, self-operation-heavy) — mix-shift leg 2"
  },
  {
   "nodeId": "geo.PL",
   "op": "set",
   "value": 0.1,
   "note": "Triple-corroborated 660MW installed-load scope — mix-shift leg 3"
  }
 ],
 "indicators": [
  {
   "signal": "Publication of a consistent-scope CE country capacity series (installed IT load, all facility types)",
   "where": "EUDCA 'State of European Data Centres' country profiles; PMR commercial-DC series releases",
   "threshold": "Any table showing DE <45% of CE-7 installed load",
   "updates": "increases"
  },
  {
   "signal": "Ratio of non-colo (enterprise/self-operated) to colo capacity additions by country",
   "where": "BMWK/Bitkom German DC updates; CBRE Suisse annual data-center figures",
   "threshold": "Non-colo share of additions >40% in CH or DE",
   "updates": "increases"
  }
 ],
 "mitigations": [
  {
   "action": "Buy or build one consistent-scope installed-load table for CE-7 (the exact promotion path the ledger names but never executed) before committing country-level sales and certification spend",
   "type": "information",
   "voiNodeId": "geo.DE",
   "note": "De-risks the largest geography leaf; the same table simultaneously resolves CH and PL"
  },
  {
   "action": "Stage country entry so that certification and channel investments in PL and CH are decision-gated on the installed-load view rather than the colo view",
   "type": "operational",
   "voiNodeId": null,
   "note": "Prevents sunk GTM cost against the possibly-wrong DE-heavy map"
  }
 ],
 "lens": "ledger-self-audit",
 "riskId": "risk.ledger-self-audit.colo-denominator-misallocates-geography"
}

VALIDATION ERROR:
[
  {
    "code": "invalid_value",
    "values": [
      "error",
      "risk"
    ],
    "path": [
      0,
      "resolution"
    ],
    "message": "Invalid option: expected one of \"error\"|\"risk\""
  }
]

Emit the corrected hypothesis JSON (same shape).

PMR commercial-DC series releases","threshold":"Any table showing DE <45% of CE-7 installed load","updates":"increases"},{"signal":"Ratio of non-colo (enterprise/self-operated) to colo capacity additions by country","where":"BMWK/Bitkom German DC updates;
